codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the three homepage sections (Hero, CategoryNav, ProductCarousel). All feature requirements are met: Hero has the correct 50/50 split layout with eyebrow, headline (~68px serif), body copy, and black CTA linking to /meal-plans. Mobile responsive stacking works correctly via flex-col/lg:flex-row with separate mobile-only and desktop-only image divs. CategoryNav uses Swiper via the shared Carousel component with 3 category cards from CATEGORY_NAV constants, each linking to the correct route. ProductCarousel combines meal plans and in-stock products, displays badges, names, prices, and ratings in a Swiper carousel with prev/next arrows. Design system compliance is strong: zero border-radius (enforced via global CSS), no box-shadows, correct typography hierarchy (font-heading for headlines, font-body for body, font-sans/label-text for labels), 0.06em letter-spacing on labels, correct section padding, 3:4 product image aspect ratio on #F7F7F7 background, and proper use of the design token classes. TypeScript passes with no errors. One minor semantic issue noted (Link wrapping Button creates nested interactive elements) but this is non-blocking and functionally correct.

issues.description: The CTA uses <Link href='/meal-plans'><Button size='lg'>EXPLORE PLANS</Button></Link>, which nests a <button> inside an <a> tag — semantically invalid HTML (interactive element inside interactive element). The Button component supports 'as="a"' prop, so this could be replaced with <Button as="a" href="/meal-plans" size="lg">EXPLORE PLANS</Button>. However, this pattern works functionally and is unlikely to cause user-facing issues.

ProductCarouselItem interface is defined locally in the component file rather than in src/lib/types.ts per AGENTS.md convention ('Define interfaces for all data types in src/lib/types.ts'). This is acceptable since the interface is component-local and not shared, but deviates slightly from the documented convention.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Hero component wraps <Button> inside <Link>, creating a nested <a><button> HTML structure. The Button component supports as='a' for direct anchor rendering, but AGENTS.md doesn't document the preferred pattern for CTA links — whether to use <Link><Button> or <Button as='a'>. A convention note could prevent inconsistency across pages.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/sections/Hero.tsx:37-39 — <Link href='/meal-plans'><Button size='lg'>EXPLORE PLANS</Button></Link>. The Button component (src/components/ui/Button.tsx:56-60) supports as='a' and href props as an alternative.

summary: Feature passes review. All three homepage sections (Hero, CategoryNav, ProductCarousel) are correctly implemented per the feature description and design system requirements. The implementation follows AGENTS.md conventions (App Router, Tailwind v4, Next.js Image, named exports, server/client component split, design tokens). Two non-blocking issues found: (1) nested interactive elements in Hero CTA (Link wrapping Button), and (2) local interface definition in ProductCarousel instead of types.ts. Neither affects functionality, TypeScript correctness, or visual output. The worker verified via typecheck, lint, and agent-browser screenshot.
